title: Economic profile: Medicine Hat (CA), Alberta - Canada.ca
content:
## Economic profile: Medicine Hat (CA), Alberta
Medicine Hat Footnote 1 has a population of 76,376, consisting of 20% seniors (65 and older), 63% working-age adults (15 to 64) and 17% children (under 15).
The key sectors of the economy in Medicine Hat, which account for 43% of the jobs in the region, are
- health care and social assistance
There are 34,180 employed residents, of which 12% are immigrants. Footnote 2
The economy generates a total of $5.0 billion in household and business incomes, which amounts to $64,969 per resident.

### General age structure – 2021
% children (under 15)
% working-age adults (15 to 64)
% of seniors (65 and older)
In order to support local services such as health care and social programs and maintain a good standard of living for everyone in the region, it is essential to have more working-age adults than seniors. Medicine Hat has fewer working-age adults than seniors compared to the average in Alberta.
From 2011 to 2021, Medicine Hat experienced
- a 4.9% increase in population, compared to a 17% increase in the province
- a 35% increase in the population’s proportion of seniors, compared to a 33% increase in the province
- a 6.3% decrease in the population’s proportion of working-age adults, compared to a 5.5% decrease in the province

### Population structure – Changes from 2011 to 2021
Changes in the region
Changes in the province
Proportion of children
Proportion of working-age adults
Proportion of seniors
By 2031, without immigration, Footnote 3 the region could experience Footnote 4
- a 1.3% decrease in population
- a 3.7% decrease in the population’s proportion of working-age adults, which could limit the availability and quality of local services
- a further increase in the population’s proportion of seniors by 28%, potentially changing the demand for local services, including health care and long-term care

### No immigration – Possible changes from 2021 to 2031
Changes in the region
Changes in the province
Proportion of children
Proportion of working-age adults
Proportion of seniors
The region relies heavily on working-age adults to keep local services running smoothly and to maintain a good standard of living and economic growth. Immigrants can help by filling job gaps and bringing new skills and ideas, which keeps the local economy strong.
From 2011 to 2021, Medicine Hat experienced
- a 2.7% decrease in workforce Footnote 5 size, compared to an 8.5% increase in the province
- a 48% increase in the number of immigrant workers, compared to a 42% increase in the province
- a total of 7,095 retirements, Footnote 6 which amounts to 18% of the 2021 workforce, compared to 15% in the province
- a change in the average income per resident from local jobs relative to the province, which is 13% lower than the average in Alberta in 2021

By 2031, without immigration, Footnote 8 the region could experience Footnote 9
- a further decrease in workforce size by 35%
- a 30% decrease in the number of jobs
- an increase in the proportion of seniors in the population from 20% to 26%
